name: aws-secrets-rotation
description: Automate AWS secrets rotation for RDS, API keys, and credentials
capabilities: Create and manage secrets in AWS Secrets Manager via CLI commands
Deploy Lambda functions for automatic RDS password rotation
Implement custom rotation logic for third-party API keys like Stripe
Configure CloudWatch alarms to monitor rotation failures
Generate compliance reports tracking rotation schedules
Execute emergency rotation for compromised credentials
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 70 static findings are false positives. The skill contains legitimate AWS CLI documentation, Lambda rotation code examples, and compliance tracking scripts. External command patterns are bash examples in markdown code blocks demonstrating AWS API usage. Network references are official AWS and Stripe API endpoints. No malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Manual secrets rotation is error-prone and often neglected. This skill provides production-ready Lambda functions and AWS CLI commands to automate credential rotation for databases and third-party services.
userTitle: Automate AWS Secrets Rotation for RDS and API Keys
limitations: Requires AWS credentials with Secrets Manager and Lambda permissions
Custom API key rotation requires API support for key regeneration
RDS rotation requires appropriate database user privileges
Does not support on-premises or non-AWS secret stores

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Automating Database Credential Rotation
useCases.description: Set up 30-day automatic rotation for production RDS MySQL credentials using AWS-managed Lambda templates with CloudWatch monitoring for rotation failures.

useCases.title: Security Team Implementing Compliance Requirements
useCases.description: Deploy rotation policies for all secrets, generate quarterly compliance reports showing rotation status, and configure alerts for overdue rotations.

useCases.title: Developer Rotating Third-Party API Keys
useCases.description: Create custom Lambda function to rotate Stripe API keys by calling their API, validating new keys, and revoking old credentials automatically.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Secret Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an AWS secret for my production MySQL database with username admin, host mydb.cluster-abc.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com, port 3306, and database myapp
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Setting up your first secret

promptTemplates.title: Enable RDS Automatic Rotation
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up automatic rotation every 30 days for my RDS MySQL secret using the AWS-managed Lambda rotation function

promptTemplates.title: Custom API Key Rotation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Lambda function that rotates Stripe API keys by calling the Stripe API to generate new keys, test them, and revoke old ones

promptTemplates.title: Compliance Audit Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a compliance report listing all secrets without rotation enabled and those not rotated in over 90 days

bestPractices: Test rotation in non-production environments before deploying to production
Configure CloudWatch alarms to alert on rotation failures within 5 minutes
Maintain runbooks documenting emergency rotation procedures for compromised credentials
antiPatterns: Hardcoding secrets in application code instead of retrieving from Secrets Manager
Setting rotation intervals longer than 90 days for sensitive credentials
Rotating secrets without testing application compatibility first

faq.question: What AWS permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need secretsmanager:CreateSecret, secretsmanager:GetSecretValue, secretsmanager:RotateSecret, secretsmanager:UpdateSecretVersionStage, lambda:InvokeFunction, and cloudwatch:PutMetricAlarm permissions.

faq.question: Can this rotate secrets for non-AWS databases?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create custom Lambda functions for any database or service that supports programmatic credential rotation via API.

faq.question: What happens if rotation fails?
faq.answer: The secret remains unchanged with the previous credentials. CloudWatch alarms can notify you of failures. The pending version is not promoted to current.

faq.question: How do I rotate a secret immediately after a breach?
faq.answer: Use the rotate-secret command with --rotate-immediately flag to force rotation regardless of the scheduled interval.

faq.question: Can applications access both old and new credentials during rotation?
faq.answer: Yes, AWS Secrets Manager supports multiple version stages (AWSPENDING, AWSCURRENT, AWSPREVIOUS) allowing gradual credential transitions.

faq.question: Is there a cost for using automatic rotation?
faq.answer: Yes, you pay for Lambda invocations (approximately monthly per secret) plus Secrets Manager API calls. Rotation Lambda runs cost about $0.20-0.50 per secret per month.
